Investment Analysis 8 min read

Would South Africa Be the Safest Place If World War III Broke Out?

International media ranks South Africa 11th among the safest countries. This article rationally analyzes South Africa's advantages and investment value from perspectives of geography, political neutrality, and resource self-sufficiency.

March 22, 2026 Leo Pan

As global geopolitical tensions rise, an increasing number of investors are contemplating an important question: in the event of extreme circumstances, which countries could provide a relatively safe environment? In March 2026, several international media outlets, including the Daily Mail, published a list of "Safest Countries in World War III," with South Africa ranked 11th globally—the only African nation to make the list. This selection was not made arbitrarily, but based on a comprehensive assessment of multiple objective factors including geographic location, political stance, and resource self-sufficiency capabilities.

I. Why Is South Africa on the "Safe List"?

In March 2026, multiple international media outlets evaluated the world's safest countries based on various indicators, with South Africa prominently featured. The selection criteria for this list included: geographic location, political stability, resource self-sufficiency capability, economic resilience, and distance from major conflict zones.

Three Key Factors for South Africa's Inclusion

  • Geographic Location: Located in the Southern Hemisphere, far from major conflict hotspots in the Northern Hemisphere
  • Political Neutrality: Pursues a non-aligned policy, refusing to take sides
  • Resource Self-Sufficiency: Abundant mineral and agricultural resources, reducing dependence on imports

Notably, this list is not intended to encourage panic, but rather to provide a rational perspective: in an era of globalization, risk diversification and portfolio diversification are inherently wise investment strategies. South Africa's inclusion reflects the objective advantages of its conditions, rather than a prediction of future circumstances.

II. Geographic Location: A Natural Barrier in the Southern Hemisphere

South Africa's geographic advantages are indisputable. As the southernmost country on the African continent, Cape Town is situated precisely at the confluence of the Indian and Atlantic Oceans—a true "City of Two Oceans."

Specific Distance Advantages

  • Distance from Taiwan: Approximately 12,000 km, approximately 14 hours flight time
  • Distance from Tokyo: Approximately 13,500 km
  • Distance from London: Approximately 9,000 km
  • Distance from Moscow: Approximately 10,000 km
  • Distance from major Middle Eastern cities: Over 7,000 km

Such geographic distances mean that even if significant conflicts occur in the Northern Hemisphere, South Africa can maintain relative physical security. More importantly, South Africa is located in the Southern Hemisphere's temperate zone, unaffected by the direct impact of extreme climates at the Arctic or equator—another important advantage in the context of global climate change.

III. Political Neutrality: The Wisdom of Non-Alignment

South Africa's foreign policy can be summarized in one word: "non-alignment." As a member of BRICS, South Africa maintains close economic and trade relations with China, Russia, India, and Brazil; simultaneously, South Africa is also one of the African continent's countries with the closest cooperation with the United States and the European Union.

Flexible and Pragmatic Diplomatic Stance

In January 2026, South Africa participated in a joint maritime military exercise with China, Iran, and Russia, but explicitly positioned itself as an "observer role," not participating in any offensive operations. This "participation without commitment" strategy is the embodiment of South Africa's diplomatic wisdom.

BRICS and Non-Alignment

South Africa is one of the founding members of BRICS, but this identity does not represent a military alliance. BRICS is essentially an economic cooperation platform that emphasizes a multipolar world order, rather than confrontation with specific countries. This allows South Africa to maintain flexible space amid great power competition.

IV. Expert Perspective: A Rational View of Safety Advantages

South African news media The Citizen recently interviewed international relations analyst Dr. Ahmed Jazbhay, who provided an in-depth and objective analysis of South Africa's security position:

"South Africa poses no direct political or military threat to current conflict parties. South Africa's non-aligned stance, regional focus, and lack of direct military intervention do indeed reduce immediate risks."

However, Dr. Jazbhay also reminds us to remain rational:

"Geographic distance offers limited protection and cannot defend against cyberattacks, economic disruptions, or global supply chain collapses. In our highly interconnected modern world, no country can completely isolate itself."

This balanced perspective is worth investors' deep consideration: South Africa's advantages are real, but should not be overinterpreted as "absolute safety." The smart approach is to view South Africa as part of a risk diversification strategy, rather than a foolproof safe haven.

V. Natural Resources and Food Security: The Confidence of Self-Sufficiency

South Africa is the most industrialized country in Africa, with abundant reserves of natural resources. This not only supports the national economy but also provides a certain degree of self-sufficiency capability.

Major Resource Advantages

  • Gold: Ranks among the world's top reserves, historically the world's largest gold producer
  • Platinum: Holds approximately 70% of global platinum group metal reserves
  • Diamonds: World-renowned diamond producer
  • Coal and Iron Ore: Supporting domestic energy demand and industrial development

In terms of agriculture, South Africa is one of the few African countries capable of food self-sufficiency. Thanks to its diverse climate zones, South Africa can produce various agricultural products from temperate grains to tropical fruits. The wine regions surrounding Cape Town are globally renowned.

VI. Climate Advantages: An Ideal Environment for Living

Cape Town has a typical Mediterranean climate, with warm, dry summers and mild, wet winters. Average annual temperatures range between 15-25°C, with abundant sunshine year-round, earning it the nickname "Europe of Africa."

This climate is not only suitable for living but also favorable for agricultural development. Against the backdrop of global climate change, South Africa's relatively stable climate conditions become another hidden advantage. Compared to other regions facing threats from extreme weather, South Africa's climate resilience is stronger.

VII. Why Choose DingYao Advisory?

After understanding South Africa's various advantages, the next question is: how to invest in South Africa safely and compliantly? This is where DingYao Advisory's expertise lies.

DingYao's Five Core Advantages

Professional Team

Deep understanding of the South African market, providing professional investment advice and local insights

Local Partners

Close collaboration with South African local enterprises such as Crestline Advisory and CanvasCrest Properties

Fund Security

Standard Bank custody, investors maintain autonomous control over fund flows

Transparent Process

Fully compliant throughout, meeting FICA anti-money laundering regulations, ensuring legal and compliant investments

Long-term vision is our biggest difference from other advisors. We don't view investment as a one-time transaction, but rather help clients establish long-term asset allocation strategies. From property acquisition, rental management to immigration planning, DingYao provides full lifecycle service support.

VIII. Substantial Advantages of Investing in South Africa

Setting aside geopolitical factors, South African real estate itself is an extremely attractive investment target. Here are the key data points for the Cape Town real estate market:

Core Data for Cape Town Real Estate

  • 7% Rental Yield: Significantly higher than Taiwan's average levels, providing stable cash flow
  • 1% Vacancy Rate: An undersupplied rental market ensuring stable rental income
  • 8% Annual Appreciation: Clear long-term appreciation trend for Cape Town properties
  • Currency Advantage: Rand exchange rate fluctuations create additional profit opportunities for foreign currency investors
  • Investment Immigration Threshold: Approximately TWD 22 million, more cost-effective compared to European and American countries

Ready to Explore South African Investment Opportunities?

Whether you're interested in asset allocation, retirement planning, or immigration property investment, DingYao Advisory can provide you with professional advice.

Book Free Consultation Now

Conclusion: Rational Perspective, Prudent Decision-Making

"World War III" is an extreme assumption, and we have no intention nor should we spread fear. The purpose of this article is to analyze South Africa's multiple advantages as an investment destination from an objective perspective—whether geographic, political, resource, or economic.

In today's deeply globalized world, the national boundaries of asset allocation have been broken. For Taiwanese investors, South Africa offers a unique option: enjoying investment returns higher than their home country while achieving geographic risk diversification. This is not escape, but wisdom.

If you're interested in investing in South Africa, please feel free to contact us. DingYao Advisory will assist you in achieving your global asset allocation goals with a professional, transparent, and long-term service philosophy.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Why is South Africa considered a safe country in the event of World War III?

South Africa is included in the safe list primarily based on three factors: geographic location (situated in the Southern Hemisphere, far from major conflict zones), political neutrality (pursuing a non-aligned policy), and resource self-sufficiency (abundant mineral and agricultural resources). In March 2026, multiple international media outlets ranked South Africa 11th among the safest countries.

Q: What is the investment immigration threshold for South Africa?

The investment immigration threshold for South Africa is relatively low, approximately TWD 22 million (subject to exchange rate fluctuations). Investors can apply for permanent residency through real estate or other investment forms, enjoying South Africa's quality living environment and investment opportunities.

Q: What is the investment return rate for Cape Town real estate?

Cape Town real estate investment is quite attractive: average rental yield is approximately 7%, significantly higher than Taiwan's levels; vacancy rate is only 1%, indicating strong market demand; property values appreciate by about 8% annually, coupled with Rand exchange rate advantages, creating dual profit opportunities for foreign currency investors.

References

  • World's Safest Countries If WW3 Breaks Out - Daily Mail, March 2026
  • South Africa's Role in Global Geopolitics - The Citizen, Dr Ahmed Jazbhay Interview
  • TPN Vacancy Survey Q3 2024 - TPN Credit Bureau
  • Permanent Residence Permits - Department of Home Affairs, South Africa
  • PayProp Rental Index Q1 2025 - PayProp
